title: Queue Implementation Using a Circular Array
description: A visual representation of a queue implemented with an array, showcasing the 'Front' and 'Rear' pointers, enqueue and dequeue operations, and the circular behavior to handle overflow.
relationships: 'Front' points to the first element to be removed (dequeued)
'Rear' points to the last element added (enqueued)
Enqueue adds an element at the 'Rear' and increments it
Dequeue removes an element from the 'Front' and increments it
When a pointer reaches the end of the array, it wraps around to the start (circular behavior)
